$comment: Mapping from the third-party BulletPack folder onto this engine's NATIVE strip surfaces, consumed by tools/import-bulletpack.ts. Every path is relative to the BulletPack root (argv[2], default ~/Downloads/BulletPack). This round targets the self-describing native format (design-native-format-amendment §Importer): the importer emits assets.bullets = { sheet, strips } (one packed PNG, every strip placed with x/y/frameW/frameH/frames/stride/color) plus assets.effects (one PNG per explosion strip). There is NO ship entry — player/enemy/boss 形象 are out of scope by user directive (2026-07-22); the ship seam stays engine machinery. Metadata is EXPLICIT per strip (never a filename-suffix parser — 47% of BulletPack files carry no parseable _stripN and several wide files are choice-sheets of unrelated designs, e.g. Medium_Pink_Purple and P1_Hyper_Bullet, resolved here by an explicit crop / frame count).
$grammar: The base four-stage campaign is the UNQUALIFIED consumer of the pack's baked colour (src/render/procedural.ts BULLET_VARIANTS): it fires 33 tint-free variant names of the form <baseCell>.<family> (e.g. orb.medium.decree, needle.column, glow.small.bolt), and nativeBulletAtlas keeps a pack strip named by one of those names over the floor-cell alias. So a baked design REACHES the four-stage game the moment its strip is keyed by the name that family fires — no companion content pack needed. THREE tiers (amendment §Naming, as evolved by BULLET_VARIANTS): (1) FLOOR — the 16 built-in BULLET_CELLS: whitened to luminance (rgb→255, alpha kept) so the base campaign's per-instance hue tint still colour-codes the shared cells, declared color:'tinted', animation frames PRESERVED, each fit to a per-cell coherent size (NOT the retired uniform 28px regrid). (2) VARIANTS — enemy/boss fired names / (3) SHOTS — player weapon+option fired names: the coloured native art declared color:'baked' at PIXEL-EXACT native size (no whiten, no resample; only lossless crop / 90° rotation). Because base content fires all 33 variant names TINT-FREE, baking one colour per name is safe — the identity-white default tint multiplies through unchanged. COVERAGE IS PARTIAL AND DELIBERATE: a fired name is covered here only when a BulletPack design fits its orientation and size — directional families (needle/kunai/scale + player shots, orientToHeading) take elongated line/shot art rotated to +x (rule 7); radial families take compact round/oval art whose native size stays near a bullet's (≤~45px). The 11 fired names left uncovered keep aliasing to their reskinned floor cell (a proper BulletPack shape at a coherent size), and BulletPack's oversized beams (massive/streak/long lines) and its surplus per-level player shots have no bullet-sized home this round — they stage in extra/ for the laser and weapon rounds. EFFECTS — only the two explosions that reskin a death-site floor name (burst, burst.big) ship; the other six explosion strips reach no emit site (firing a pack-only fx name would crash the never-blocked floor), so they stage until a death/bomb tier fires them.
$variants: Enemy/boss fired names (src/render/procedural.ts BULLET_VARIANTS). Directional families set orientToHeading in base content, so their art is elongated and points +x; radial families keep native orientation. Each name here is one a real four-stage playthrough fires, so the baked design reaches play.

needle.vigil.note: sentinel 'Vigil Unbroken' beam — directional green line (+x)

needle.subpoena.note: stage-3 summons homing — directional yellow line (+x)

orb.small.writ.note: stage-3/4 aimed shot (most-fired) — one 13px round design cropped from a 306×17 choice-sheet

$shots: Player weapon and option fired names (BULLET_VARIANTS player section). Directional shots are drawn nose-up in BulletPack and rotated 90° to +x (rule 7) so orientToHeading points them along the firing heading; the radial spread bolt and the option shots keep native orientation.

glow.small.bolt.note: GUN_BOLT spread — radial animated amber spiky ball

variantsDuplicate.$comment: Files that are byte-for-byte duplicates of a consumed source; represented by the named variant, not packed twice.

$effects: Only names a real death site fires ship as pack effects. `burst` (enemy death) and `burst.big` (boss/player death) RESKIN procedural fx-floor names the base campaign already emits (src/game/run.ts; effectAtlas composites a reskinned floor name from the pack's native pixels — src/render/procedural.ts nativeEffectAtlas). The base effect's `life` is fixed (burst 24, burst.big 36 ticks; src/sim/effects.ts), so ticksPerFrame lands the animation near that window. The other six explosion strips are NOT mapped: firing a pack-only fx name from a base death site would crash the never-blocked floor (main.ts:608 throws on an unresolved sprite), so they have no consumer until a death/bomb tier with its own procedural floor is added. They stage in extra/explosions/ and are counted staged, not consumed.

burst.note: RESKIN of the enemy-death flash (base effect 'burst', life 24)

burst.big.note: RESKIN of the boss/player-death flash (base effect 'burst.big', life 36): expanding shockwave
